title: "Jesus Was In A Secret Occult Group!" -Top Researcher Brian Muraresku
summary: Brian Muraresku discusses his New York Times bestselling book 'The Immortality Key,' which explores the hypothesis that ritualized psychedelic use played a foundational role in Western civilization and early Christianity. The conversation covers the Eleusinian Mysteries, the potential psychedelic nature of ancient Greek religious practices, and how these traditions may have influenced early Christian sacraments.

acronyms.definition: U.S. federal agency responsible for regulating drugs and therapeutic substances
A mental health condition triggered by experiencing or witnessing traumatic events

events.description: Reverend Martin Luther King Jr. wrote about the influence of mystery religions on Christianity
Diana Pasulka's theory identifies Roswell as a dividing line between pre-modern religious visions (angels, demons) and modern alien sightings
Roman Emperor Valentinian outlawed nocturnal rites and attempted to shut down the Eleusinian mysteries

key_claims.claim: The kykeon drink consumed at the Eleusinian Mystery rituals contained ergot, a fungus that grows on wheat and contains compounds related to LSD
Spilling the secrets of what happened at Eleusis was punishable by death
Moses's burning bush could have been a DMT-rich acacia tree
Jesus used the Greek word 'musterion' (mystery) to describe his teachings, defined as a religious secret only to be communicated to the initiated
Psilocybin silences activity in the default mode network of the brain, which is where the ego is housed
Pre-1947, people saw angels, demons, and folkloric beings; post-1947, people began seeing aliens as the modern mythos
Leslie Kane is the New York Times journalist who has broken most major UFO stories over the last four years
